deep_dive.title: Pythagoras-Prover: Advancing Efficient Formal Proving via Augmented Lean Formalisation
deep_dive.abstract: Modern Lean theorem provers achieve strong performance only with substantial training and inference compute, driven in part by scarce verified proof data and the long reasoning traces of formal proof search, making both supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and sampling expensive. We introduce Pythagoras-Prover, a compute-efficient open-source family of Lean theorem provers built for practical compute budgets. The family spans two generation paradigms: autoregressive models at 4B and 32B parameters, and a first proof-of-concept diffusion-based prover (4B) that iteratively refines Lean proofs at inference time. For training efficiency, we build a Lean-verified corpus stratified into easy, medium, and hard problems for curriculum SFT, so models acquire proof skills progressively from shorter, simpler proofs to longer, harder ones. During SFT, a dynamic proof-reasoning filtering scheme preserves informative proof traces while keeping each instance within an 8k-token context budget. We also introduce Augmented Lean Formalisation (ALF), which expands scarce verified corpora into variants of formal statements, populated via self-distillation for extra training signal without formally verifying every mutated instance. By perturbing known problems while preserving their formal character, ALF reduces reliance on any statement's surface form. Empirically, Pythagoras-Prover-4B surpasses DeepSeek-Prover-V2-671B at pass@32 on MiniF2F-Test (86.1% vs 82.4%) with ~167x fewer parameters, while Pythagoras-Prover-32B sets the open-source state of the art at 93.0% on MiniF2F-Test and solves 93 of 672 PutnamBench problems. We release MiniF2F-ALF, an ALF-mutated contamination-sensitive benchmark on which every evaluated model loses accuracy; here our 32B remains strongest and our 4B matches the prior state of the art, Goedel-Prover-V2-32B.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based theorem prover for Lean, directly applicable to hardware formal verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的Lean定理证明器，可应用于硬件形式验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的Lean定理证明器，可应用于硬件形式验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的Lean定理证明器，可应用于硬件形式验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Pythagoras-Prover introduces compute-efficient Lean theorem provers using autoregressive and diffusion models, with curriculum SFT on a stratified proof corpus, advancing formal proof automation for hardware verification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Pythagoras-Prover提出了计算高效的Lean定理证明器家族，包括自回归模型（4B和32B参数）和首个基于扩散的证明器（4B）。通过构建分层的策略SFT语料库进行课程学习，显著降低了证明搜索的计算开销，为硬件形式验证中的定理证明提供了高效自动化方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: Pythagoras-Prover提出了计算高效的Lean定理证明器家族，包括自回归模型（4B和32B参数）和首个基于扩散的证明器（4B）。通过构建分层的策略SFT语料库进行课程学习，显著降低了证明搜索的计算开销，为硬件形式验证中的定理证明提供了高效自动化方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Can LLMs assist in model checking and theorem proving for hardware design?

deep_dive.title: Structured Testbench Generation for LLM-Driven HDL Design and Verification-Oriented Data Curation
deep_dive.abstract: Automated testbench generation has become a critical bottleneck in large language model (LLM)-driven Register Transfer Level (RTL) workflows, where large numbers of candidate designs must be verified rapidly and reliably. Existing prompt-based approaches treat testbench generation as unconstrained code synthesis, yielding stochastic outputs with high token cost, low reproducibility, and insufficient coverage. To address this gap, we present STG, a Structured Testbench Generation framework that exploits the inherent structure of hardware designs to generate deterministic testbenches. As a direct verification tool, STG runs 720x faster than an iterative LLM-based testbench generation flow and higher rate of successful compilation, achieves higher coverage, and reduces false-pass verdicts on incorrect DUTs. STG also helps identify errors in RTL generation benchmarks by exposing faulty benchmark testbenches. As a data curation engine, it is 11x faster than LLM-based filtering on a single CPU core with 127x less energy, and the resulting distilled models provide state-of-the-art performance in our multi-benchmark evaluation. As a test-time scaling oracle, it reduces node count by 14-47\%. Our models are available at https://huggingface.co/collections/AS-SiliconMind/siliconmind-v12.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven RTL verification - proposes structured testbench generation for LLM-based hardware design workflows.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 大语言模型驱动的RTL验证——提出结构化测试台生成方法用于基于LLM的硬件设计流程。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 大语言模型驱动的RTL验证——提出结构化测试台生成方法用于基于LLM的硬件设计流程。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 大语言模型驱动的RTL验证——提出结构化测试台生成方法用于基于LLM的硬件设计流程。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents STG, a structured testbench generation framework that exploits hardware design structure to generate deterministic testbenches for LLM-driven RTL workflows, achieving 720x speedup over iterative LLM-based generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型（LLM）驱动RTL设计中测试台生成的瓶颈问题，本文提出结构化测试台生成（STG）框架。STG利用硬件设计的固有结构生成确定性测试台，避免了现有提示式方法的高令牌成本、低可重复性和覆盖不足。作为直接验证工具，STG比迭代式LLM生成流程快720倍，且编译成功率更高，有效支持大规模硬件设计验证。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型（LLM）驱动RTL设计中测试台生成的瓶颈问题，本文提出结构化测试台生成（STG）框架。STG利用硬件设计的固有结构生成确定性测试台，避免了现有提示式方法的高令牌成本、低可重复性和覆盖不足。作为直接验证工具，STG比迭代式LLM生成流程快720倍，且编译成功率更高，有效支持大规模硬件设计验证。

quick_skim.title: LongRTL: Graph-Similarity-Guided LLM-driven Long Context RTL Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) show great promise in RTL code generation and optimization. However, real-world RTL designs are typically long, entangled, and poorly modularized, posing a major challenge due to context-length limitations and lack of structure. To overcome these obstacles, we propose a scalable LLM-based RTL optimization framework guided by graph similarity. Our method introduces three collaborative agents: (1) a Partition Agent that decomposes RTL designs into semantically meaningful AST subtrees, guided by AST graph similarity to reusable design templates; (2) an Optimization Agent that generates RTL submodule code based on partitioned subtrees using multi-modal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with both AST and RTL guidance; and (3) a Reconstruction Agent that reassembles optimized submodules based on logic-aware ordering and Graph-RAG prompting, ensuring global functional equivalence. Together, these components enable robust, structure-aware optimization of long-context RTL designs, bridging the gap between toy examples and industrial-scale hardware codebases.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLMs applied to RTL optimization, not verification
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LongRTL uses LLMs with graph similarity to partition and optimize long RTL designs, showing potential for RTL verification tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出LongRTL框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）和图相似性引导，将长RTL设计分解为语义子模块并进行优化。通过协作智能体和检索增强生成，解决了LLM上下文长度限制和RTL结构混乱的问题。实验表明该方法可扩展至工业级设计，尽管聚焦于优化，其技术对RTL形式验证具有迁移潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出LongRTL框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）和图相似性引导，将长RTL设计分解为语义子模块并进行优化。通过协作智能体和检索增强生成，解决了LLM上下文长度限制和RTL结构混乱的问题。实验表明该方法可扩展至工业级设计，尽管聚焦于优化，其技术对RTL形式验证具有迁移潜力。

quick_skim.title: OpenRTLSet: A Fully Open-Source Dataset for Large Language Model-based Verilog Module Design
quick_skim.abstract: OpenRTLSet introduces the largest fully open-source dataset for hardware design, offering over 131,000 diverse Verilog code samples to the research community and industry. Our dataset uniquely combines Verilog code from GitHub repositories (102k modules), VHDL translations (5k modules), and synthesizable C/C++ translations (24k modules), all freely accessible without proprietary restrictions. Using the reasoning model DeepSeek-R1, we generated paired natural language descriptions for each code sample, enabling fine-tuning of various language model families (e.g., Qwen and Granite) for Verilog code generation. Our dataset explores multiple options, including Verilator-generated C++ files as additional context during labeling, quantization techniques (INT4 vs. BF16), and performance differences across model sizes (7B-32B parameters). OpenRTLSet demonstrates that open-source approaches can achieve superior performance in hardware design tasks, establishing a new foundation for accessible research and commercial use in this domain.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM dataset for Verilog design, can be leveraged for RTL verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供LLM用于Verilog设计的数据集，可辅助RTL验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供LLM用于Verilog设计的数据集，可辅助RTL验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供LLM用于Verilog设计的数据集，可辅助RTL验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: OpenRTLSet provides the largest open-source Verilog dataset with over 131k modules, generated natural language descriptions using DeepSeek-R1, enabling fine-tuning of LLMs for Verilog code generation, which can be leveraged for RTL verification tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: OpenRTLSet发布了最大的完全开源硬件设计数据集，包含超过13万条Verilog代码样本，并利用DeepSeek-R1模型为每条代码生成自然语言描述。该数据集支持微调多种语言模型用于Verilog代码生成，为LLM在RTL验证中的应用提供了基础设施。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: OpenRTLSet发布了最大的完全开源硬件设计数据集，包含超过13万条Verilog代码样本，并利用DeepSeek-R1模型为每条代码生成自然语言描述。该数据集支持微调多种语言模型用于Verilog代码生成，为LLM在RTL验证中的应用提供了基础设施。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索关于大语言模型在硬件形式验证中应用的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: large language model for hardware formal verification; hardware formal verification using large language models; RTL verification with LLMs; What are the latest advances in applying LLMs to formal verification of RTL designs?; Can LLMs assist in model checking and theorem proving for hardware design?.

quick_skim.title: Specifying Hardware Communication as Programs
quick_skim.abstract: To test and debug hardware modules, it is common to write two programs: a driver, which translates high-level transactions into interactions on the module's input and output signals, and a monitor, which analyzes a signal-level execution trace and recognizes a transaction. These two programs are commonly implemented separately for each hardware protocol, but this separation entails manual effort and risks inconsistencies.   We advocate an alternative approach. We present a DSL in which users specify hardware communication protocols as succinct imperative programs. Crucially, the same specification can be used to both drive designs and monitor transactions. We present the design of a tool, which given a specification in our DSL and a waveform, automatically infers a transaction-level trace consistent with the waveform. We discuss plans to evaluate our DSL on real-world interconnects such as Wishbone and AXI-Stream.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hardware verification techniques for digital circuits - uses DSL for driving and monitoring hardware modules
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 数字电路硬件验证技术——使用DSL驱动和监控硬件模块
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 数字电路硬件验证技术——使用DSL驱动和监控硬件模块
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 数字电路硬件验证技术——使用DSL驱动和监控硬件模块
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a DSL to specify hardware communication protocols as programs, enabling unified driving and monitoring of hardware modules for testing and debugging.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一种特定领域语言（DSL），用于将硬件通信协议规范化为简洁的命令式程序。同一个规范既可用于驱动硬件设计，也可用于监控事务，克服了传统分别编写驱动和监控程序带来的手动工作和不一致性问题。该工具能够根据波形自动推断事务，从而高效测试和调试硬件模块。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出一种特定领域语言（DSL），用于将硬件通信协议规范化为简洁的命令式程序。同一个规范既可用于驱动硬件设计，也可用于监控事务，克服了传统分别编写驱动和监控程序带来的手动工作和不一致性问题。该工具能够根据波形自动推断事务，从而高效测试和调试硬件模块。
